subject: DevOps
title: Tagging Standards
description: A short lesson to help you apply Tagging Standards.

hook.text: Who owns this EC2 instance? What project is it for? Is it dev or prod? Without tags, nobody knows.

buildup.text: Tags are metadata labels on cloud resources: team, environment, project, cost center, owner.

discovery.text: IaC enforces tagging. Every resource in the code includes mandatory tags. No tags, no deploy.

twist.text: Tags power cost reports, security audits, and cleanup. 'env=dev' lets you auto-delete dev resources nightly.

climax.text: Tag policies in AWS Organizations enforce standards across accounts. Non-compliant resources get flagged.

punchline.text: Tags classify resources. IaC enforces them. Cost and security depend on it.

quiz.question: Why are consistent tagging standards important in cloud infrastructure?
options: They enable cost tracking, security audits, and automated cleanup
They are required by the operating system
